name: Revere Firefighters
description_short: Revere Firefighters Federal Credit Union is a small, well-capitalized credit union chartered in 1957, serving 222 members with $5.89M in assets from a single location in Revere, MA.
description_long: Revere Firefighters Federal Credit Union was established in 1957 and holds a state charter with NCUA oversight. The credit union is headquartered at 199 Revere Street in Revere, Massachusetts, and operates as a member-owned financial cooperative specifically serving firefighters and eligible community members in the Revere area. The credit union offers traditional banking services including savings and deposit accounts, consumer loans (auto loans and mortgages), and investment products. With $5.89 million in total assets as of Q1 2024, the institution maintains a loan portfolio of $1.75 million and total member deposits of $5.15 million. The credit union operates a single branch location with three part-time employees and no ATM network. Revere Firefighters FCU distinguishes itself through its specialized field of membership focused on the firefighting community and its strong capital position. With a net worth ratio of 13.54, the credit union is classified as "well-capitalized" — significantly above the 7.0 threshold required by the NCUA. This strong capital base provides members with financial security and stability typical of well-managed cooperative institutions. As a small, community-based credit union, this institution is best suited for local Revere firefighters and eligible members seeking personalized service and member-owned banking. The limited branch footprint (1 location, 0 ATMs) and minimal staffing (3 part-time employees) mean members should expect traditional banking hours and limited digital/mobile capabilities. The institution's small asset base and membership of 222 members reflects its niche, specialized market position rather than broad consumer appeal.
services: Member savings and deposit accounts with NCUA insurance coverage
Auto loans with monthly trend tracking and delinquency reporting
Credit union membership with voting rights
Financial performance reporting and transparency documentation
Member account routing number services (ABA Routing: 211385365)
pros: Well-capitalized with 13.54 net worth ratio, significantly above the 7.0 regulatory threshold for financial strength
Member-owned credit union structure provides alignment with member interests versus shareholder profit maximization
Long history of service since 1957 demonstrates institutional stability and longevity
Offers auto loans and mortgages with specific lending data tracked and reported to regulatory authorities
NCUA-insured member accounts and deposits provide federal insurance protection
Specialized field of membership serves firefighting community cohesively
Maintains positive net income ($12,797 in Q1 2024) demonstrating operational profitability
cons: Single branch location in Revere with zero ATM network limits accessibility and convenience for members
Only 3 part-time employees suggest limited customer service capacity and hours of operation
Very small institution (222 members, $5.89M assets) may lack investment in modern digital/mobile banking platforms
Limited field of membership eligibility restricts access to only firefighters and select community members
No published information on fees, rates, loan terms, or service offerings makes comparison difficult for prospective members
best_for: Revere-area firefighters seeking member-owned banking with community focus
Individuals eligible for firefighter field of membership wanting personalized local service
Members comfortable with traditional banking requiring only basic checking, savings, and loan products
Community members prioritizing capital strength and financial stability over digital convenience
diagnosis: Revere Firefighters Federal Credit Union is best for local firefighters in the Revere, Massachusetts area seeking stable, member-owned banking with a strong capital position. The main caveat is that this is an extremely small institution with minimal staffing and no ATM network, so members should expect traditional banking hours and limited digital capabilities, and should verify services and eligibility by calling (781) 853-0660 before joining.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
